description: Neutral 6cl7 refined against a simulated 2.0 A ED dataset with approximately 20% Rmerge proxy and 100 nm empirical thickness effects.

notes: Dataset was generated earlier in the Charges workspace stress test. To be made public later.
Use your own model and MTZ when moving this standalone hack into a separate repository.

commands.standard_phenix_reference: phenix.refine reference_model.pdb simulated_obs.mtz data_manager.model.type=electron data_manager.miller_array.labels.name=FSIM,SIGFSIM data_manager.miller_array.labels.name=FREE refinement.main.scattering_table=electron refinement.main.number_of_macro_cycles=3 refinement.output.write_maps=False refinement.output.write_map_coefficients=True overwrite=true output.prefix=phenix_run
commands.mb_wrapper: bin/phenix.refine.mb --mb-xray-table wk1995 --mb-electron-voltage-kv 200 reference_model.pdb simulated_obs.mtz data_manager.model.type=electron data_manager.miller_array.labels.name=FSIM,SIGFSIM data_manager.miller_array.labels.name=FREE refinement.main.scattering_table=electron refinement.main.number_of_macro_cycles=3 refinement.output.write_maps=False refinement.output.write_map_coefficients=True overwrite=true output.prefix=phenix_mb_run

interpretation: The neutral-atom Mott-Bethe wrapper completed a full 6cl7 refinement on this noisy example dataset.
In this specific example the final R_work matched the ordinary Phenix run to four decimal places, while R_free was slightly lower for the wrapper.
This is one example, not a benchmark across many datasets.The MB wrapper is slightly slower.
